ownership_basis.contracts_root_policy: contracts/ owns machine-readable, version-pinned, compatibility-sensitive authority surfaces.
ownership_basis.projection_doctrine: IDE projects and generated manifests remain projections; source schema/examples are authoritative projection metadata, not generated output.
ownership_basis.slot_gap: contracts/projections/ide does not exist yet and ownership_slots.toml has no projection_contract slot. The later apply task must create the scoped contract path and record the slot decision without changing schema semantics.

rejected_owners.reason: IDE is the projection family qualifier, but projection contract meaning is the stronger owner.
CMake and scripts consume and generate manifests, but the tracked schema/examples define metadata shape rather than tool implementation.
The files are machine-readable contract metadata, not explanatory prose.
The files are tracked source metadata and examples, not generated proof output.
Preservation keeps the transitional ide root active even though a contract/projection owner can be planned.
